results.exclusion_reason: ILO Eligibility: The guidelines define a strict and exhaustive list of eligible lead applicants (NGOs, private companies, public sector operators, local authorities, or inter-governmental organizations as defined by Article 159(1) of the EU Financial Regulation). While ILO as an inter-governmental organization could technically fall under Article 159(1), the guidelines also require the lead applicant to be 'registered towards the relevant authority in Uganda i.e. legitimated to fully operate in Uganda' and 'effectively established in a Member State of the European Union or in an eligible country or territory as defined under Article 28(1) of NDICI-GE' — though the latter obligation does not apply to international organisations. However, more critically, the guidelines require the lead applicant to demonstrate at least 7 years of experience in incubation and acceleration of MSMEs preferably in NTFPs Value Chains and/or Rural Development, and the capacity to manage a yearly project portfolio of at least EUR 1,000,000. These are specialized sectoral requirements (non-timber forest products value chains, MSME incubation) outside the ILO's core mandate. Additionally, the CfP is focused on forest conservation and non-timber value chain enterprise development — a domain not squarely within ILO's mandate. The primary exclusion reason is that while ILO could be considered an inter-governmental organization eligible in principle, the operational eligibility requirements (NTFP/MSME incubation expertise, Uganda registration) make ILO an implausible fit, and the ILO match score is very low.
results.classification_summary: This is a restricted Call for Proposals by AICS (Italian Agency for Development Cooperation) Kampala office, funded by the EU under the ReForEst Uganda project (NDICI AFRICA/2023/450-049). It seeks one grantee to implement Output 3 of the ReForEst Uganda project: developing sustainable non-timber forest product (NTFP) value chain enterprises in Eastern Uganda and Napak District, Karamoja. The grant range is EUR 3,000,000 to EUR 4,000,000. Eligible lead applicants must be NGOs, private companies, public sector operators, local authorities, or inter-governmental organizations registered in Uganda with at least 7 years of MSME incubation/acceleration experience. The deadline for concept note submission is 7 May 2026. While ILO could qualify as an inter-governmental organization under Article 159(1) of the EU Financial Regulation, the highly specialized eligibility requirements (NTFP/MSME incubation experience, Uganda registration) and the thematic focus (forest conservation, non-timber value chains) make this opportunity a poor fit for ILO. The ILO match score is very low.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The guidelines (art. 2.1.1) define eligible lead applicants as NGOs, private companies, public sector operators, local authorities, or international (inter-governmental) organizations as defined by Article 159(1) of the EU Financial Regulation — registered in Uganda and legitimated to fully operate there. While ILO is an inter-governmental organization, the guidelines also require a minimum of 7 years' experience in incubation and acceleration of MSMEs preferably in NTFPs Value Chains and/or Rural Development, and demonstration of capacity to manage a yearly project portfolio of at least EUR 1,000,000. These specialized sectoral requirements are outside ILO's mandate and expertise, effectively excluding it from eligibility as a lead applicant. ILO is not explicitly listed as an eligible entity type in the context of this specialized call.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The document explicitly states this is a Call for Proposals for a non-reimbursable grant, with amounts between EUR 3,000,000 and EUR 4,000,000.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The minimum grant amount is EUR 3,000,000 and maximum is EUR 4,000,000, both well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline for submission of concept notes is May 7th, 2026 at 16:00 EAT, which is in the future relative to today's date of April 22, 2026.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is a Call for Proposals for a grant, not a procurement or consultancy tender.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The guidelines state the grant may cover up to 100% of eligible costs. Co-financing is not mandatory; the guidelines note that if full financing is requested, the applicant must justify it, but it is not excluded.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: No specific pre-existing accreditation with another body (e.g., GEF, Green Climate Fund) is required. Note that lead applicants whose pillars have been positively assessed by the European Commission may sign a contribution agreement instead of a standard grant contract, but this is not a prerequisite for applying.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The guidelines do not specify that consortium leadership must be held by a non-UN entity. The lead applicant categories include inter-governmental organizations.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: Lead applicants must be effectively established in a Member State of the European Union or in an eligible country or territory as defined under Article 28(1) of NDICI-GE (though this does not apply to international organizations), and must be registered/legitimated to fully operate in Uganda.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), acting as contracting authority under the EU-funded ReForEst Uganda project (NDICI AFRICA/2023/450-049, total budget EUR 14 million), is issuing a restricted Call for Proposals (ReForEst/CFP/2026/03) to identify one grantee to implement Output 3 of the project: developing sustainable non-timber forest product (NTFP) value chain enterprises and strategies for enhanced livelihoods in Uganda's Eastern Region and Napak District (Karamoja). The grant amount ranges from EUR 3,000,000 to EUR 4,000,000, covering up to 100% of eligible costs. Eligible lead applicants must be NGOs, private companies, public sector operators, local authorities, or inter-governmental organizations, registered and legitimated to operate in Uganda, and effectively established in an EU Member State or NDICI-GE eligible country (exception applies to international organizations). Lead applicants must have at least 7 years of MSME incubation/acceleration experience in NTFP value chains and/or rural development. The call follows a two-stage process: concept notes are due by 7 May 2026 at 16:00 EAT, with up to 5 pre-selected applicants later invited to submit full proposals. The action targets 16 districts across Eastern Uganda and must promote 5 NTFP value chains (cashew, wild mango, honey, sheanut, bamboo), incubate 140 enterprises, support 50 production groups, and sustain 5,000 jobs.
results.ilo_match_evidence: This CfP is primarily focused on forest conservation, non-timber forest product value chain development, and MSME incubation in Uganda — areas peripheral to the ILO's core mandate of labour standards, employment policy, social protection, and social dialogue, with only minor overlap in job creation and rural livelihoods.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), Addis Ababa Office, has launched a Call for Proposals (AID 13351/01/0) for emergency humanitarian assistance in Sudan, with a total available grant of €4,500,000 covering Food Security/Nutrition, WaSH, Education in Emergencies, Health, and Protection. An additional €500,000 is allocated for management costs, bringing the total initiative budget to €5,000,000. The initiative aims to guarantee essential life-saving services for populations affected by the ongoing armed conflict in Sudan. Eligible applicants are strictly limited to non-profit civil society organizations (OSC) registered in the AICS list pursuant to Article 26 of Italian Law 125/2014, or consortia (ATS) led by such organizations or by local Sudanese organizations. The ILO and other international organizations are not eligible. The deadline for submission is 4 May 2026 at 17:00. No co-financing requirement is stated, but applicants must demonstrate operational capacity in Sudan.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While the CfP addresses humanitarian needs including food security, health, and protection in a conflict-affected country which has some tangential links to ILO's decent work in crisis mandate, it is primarily a humanitarian emergency response focused on basic survival services rather than core ILO mandate areas such as labour standards, employment, social protection systems, or social dialogue.

Deadline is future.evidence: The submission deadline was 18 March 2026 at 16:00 local time, which has already passed (today is 2026-04-22). The procedure status is listed as 'Conclusa' (concluded), and the final ranking ('Graduatoria finale') was published on 26 March 2026.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS) Beirut office launched a Call for Proposals for first emergency humanitarian assistance in Lebanon (AID 013508/01/0) in response to the conflict escalation beginning March 2026. Total available funding is EUR 6,000,000 for multisectoral emergency interventions (food security, WASH, shelter, health/nutrition, protection, MPCA, education) across Lebanon. Individual grants are capped at EUR 750,000 per project with a maximum implementation period of 4 months. Eligible applicants are exclusively non-profit civil society organisations registered in the AICS list under Italian Law 125/2014, or non-registered entities with a partnership agreement with a listed organisation, demonstrating specific experience in humanitarian aid and operational capacity in Lebanon. The ILO and other international organisations are not eligible. Co-financing is encouraged but not mandatory. The submission deadline was 18 March 2026 at 16:00 (local time), and the procedure has been concluded with the final ranking published on 26 March 2026.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While the CfP addresses humanitarian crisis response in Lebanon with some protection and social dimensions, it focuses narrowly on emergency relief (food, WASH, shelter, health) rather than ILO core mandates of labour standards, employment, social protection systems, or social dialogue.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO Eligibility: The CfP explicitly restricts eligible applicants to non-profit organizations ('soggetti non profit'), specifically civil society organizations (OSCs) registered or enrollable in the AICS list. Only non-profit organizations (OSCs/NGOs) are eligible to apply; international organizations like the ILO are excluded. The deadline for submission is 05 May 2026 at 12:00 (Niamey time). The CfP is ineligible for ILO due to the strict restriction to non-profit entities.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The CfP explicitly states (Section 5) that proposals must be submitted by 'soggetti non profit' (non-profit entities), either registered on the AICS list or meeting specific non-profit criteria. International organizations such as the ILO are not included in the exhaustive list of eligible applicants.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The CfP provides non-reimbursable grants ('contributi a dono') to selected non-profit organizations for project implementation; no loan or repayment obligation is involved.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The total initiative budget is €1,800,000 for a single lot; individual project grants can be up to €800,000 (single applicant) or €1,000,000 (ATS/consortium), both well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline for submission is 05 May 2026 at 12:00 Niamey time, which is after today's date of 22 April 2026.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is a project grant Call for Proposals, not a procurement/tender for goods or services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: No mandatory co-financing requirement is mentioned in the available CfP documents.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: Applicants must either be registered on the AICS list of non-profit organizations (art. 26, comma 3, Legge 125/2014) or meet specific eligibility criteria for non-registered entities; this constitutes a specific registration/accreditation requirement with AICS.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The CfP allows for ATS (temporary consortia of non-profits) but does not specify that a non-UN entity must lead; the restriction applies to all applicants being non-profit entities.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The CfP is addressed to Italian non-profit organizations or those registered with AICS (an Italian government agency), implying a strong preference/requirement for Italian-registered entities, as evidenced by the FAQ referencing 'OSC Italiana' and AICS registration.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), through its Niamey office, has launched a Call for Proposals for the emergency initiative 'ReSSARC: Rafforzamento del Settore Sanitario e della Sicurezza Alimentare nella Repubblica Centrafricana' (AID 013381/01/0). With a total budget of €1,800,000 (plus €200,000 in management costs), the initiative aims to strengthen health systems, combat malnutrition, and improve food security for vulnerable populations in the Central African Republic, with a focus on maternal and child health and gender-sensitive approaches. Only non-profit organizations (Italian OSCs/NGOs), either registered on the AICS list or meeting specific eligibility criteria, are eligible to apply, as single applicants (up to €800,000) or in consortium/ATS (up to €1,000,000). The deadline for submission is 5 May 2026 at 12:00 (Niamey time) via certified email (PEC). No mandatory co-financing is required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: This CfP focuses on emergency humanitarian health and food security interventions in the Central African Republic, with limited alignment to ILO's core mandate areas of labour standards, employment, social protection systems, or decent work; only tangential links through social protection and vulnerable populations.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO Eligibility: The CfP explicitly restricts eligible applicants to non-profit entities ('soggetti non profit') registered in Italy (OSC/NGOs inscribed in the AICS list per art. 26) and locally-registered non-profit organisations in Ethiopia. The ILO as an international/intergovernmental organisation does not fall within these categories.
results.classification_summary: This is a Call for Proposals by AICS (Italian Agency for Development Cooperation) for humanitarian response strengthening in Ethiopia, with a total budget of EUR 10.4 million across three components. The call is restricted to Italian-registered non-profit organisations (OSC) inscribed in the AICS list and their partners. The deadline has been extended to 27 April 2026 (still future as of today). ILO is not eligible as it is an international organisation, not a non-profit entity registered in Italy.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The CfP explicitly states it selects projects 'presentati da soggetti non profit' (submitted by non-profit entities) per art. 19 of AICS statute. Section 5 (Requisiti di partecipazione) and the FAQ make clear that eligible lead applicants must be OSC (Civil Society Organisations) inscribed in the AICS official list per art. 26, which is a registry for Italian non-profit organisations. The ILO as a UN/international intergovernmental organisation does not fit this category.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The CfP provides non-reimbursable grants ('contributi') to selected non-profit organisations for humanitarian projects. This is a standard grant instrument with no repayment obligation.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The three lots total EUR 10.4 million (Lot 1: EUR 5,200,000; Lot 2: EUR 1,500,000; Lot 3: EUR 3,700,000). Individual grants can be up to EUR 700,000 per single organisation or up to EUR 1,500,000 for joint proposals, well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline was extended to 27 April 2026 at 17:00 (Addis Abeba time), as confirmed by the extension notice document dated 20/03/2026. Today is 22 April 2026, so the deadline is still in the future.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is a Call for Proposals (CfP) for project grants, not a procurement or consultancy tender. No RFP/ITB language is used.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: No mandatory co-financing requirement is mentioned in the available CfP text or FAQ documents.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: Lead applicants must be inscribed in the official AICS list ('elenco') per art. 26, which constitutes a specific pre-existing accreditation/registration requirement with AICS.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The CfP specifies that in an ATS (Temporary Association), the lead entity (mandataria/capofila) must be an OSC inscribed in the AICS list. Developing country-based NGOs that demonstrate local operational capacity may be required to assume the lead role. These are consortium leadership rules specific to non-UN entities.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The CfP requires lead applicants (proponenti/mandatari) to be Italian non-profit organisations inscribed in the AICS list per art. 26, which implies Italian registration/legal establishment.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), through its Addis Abeba office, has issued a Call for Proposals (AID 013303/01/0) for a EUR 10.4 million humanitarian response initiative in Ethiopia titled 'Rafforzamento della risposta umanitaria in Etiopia.' The initiative is structured across three components: Component 1 (EUR 5.2M) addresses conflict-related needs in northern Ethiopia (Tigray, Afar, Amhara); Component 2 (EUR 1.5M) responds to the complex South Sudanese and Sudanese crisis and displacement in Gambella and Benishangul Gumuz; and Component 3 (EUR 3.7M) addresses extreme climate phenomena in Oromia, Somali, South West, South Ethiopia, Central, and Sidama regions. Eligible applicants are Italian non-profit civil society organisations (OSC) inscribed in the AICS official list per art. 26, with individual grants capped at EUR 700,000 per single organisation and EUR 1,500,000 for joint ATS consortia. No co-financing requirement is specified. The submission deadline has been extended to 27 April 2026 at 17:00 (Addis Abeba time). The ILO is not eligible as it is an international organisation, not an Italian-registered non-profit entity.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While this CfP addresses humanitarian response in Ethiopia, which has tangential links to ILO's work on decent work in crisis situations, it is primarily focused on food security, health, nutrition, WASH and protection activities rather than core ILO mandate areas such as labour standards, employment, social protection or social dialogue.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), through its regional office in Nairobi, issues this Call for Proposals for the FO.PR.ALB initiative — 'Quality Vocational Training for the Tourism and Hospitality Sector of the Zanzibar Archipelago' (AID 013267/01/1). The initiative aims to improve quality vocational training aligned with the needs of the tourism/hospitality labour market in Tanzania, addressing youth unemployment and skills mismatches. The total grant available is EUR 3,950,000 split into two lots: Lot 1 (EUR 2,950,000) for activities in the Zanzibar Archipelago (Unguja and Pemba islands), and Lot 2 (EUR 1,000,000) for vocational training centres in continental Tanzania with a focus on linking to Zanzibar. Eligible applicants are exclusively Italian Civil Society Organizations (OSCs) and non-profit entities registered in the AICS list under Art. 26(3) of Law 125/2014. At least one local partner operating in Tanzania is mandatory. The deadline for submission is 27 April 2026 at 16:00. No co-financing requirement is specified. The ILO is not eligible as lead applicant but is mentioned as a reference organization in the bando acronyms.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The CfP directly addresses skills development, vocational training, youth employment, labour market linkages, and decent work insertion in the tourism sector in Tanzania, aligning strongly with ILO mandates on skills development, employment policy, and decent work.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO Eligibility: The CfP explicitly restricts eligible applicants to Italian Civil Society Organizations (OSC) registered on the list under Art. 26, para. 3 of Law 125/2014 (Italian non-profit organizations). The ILO, as an international organization, does not fall within this strictly defined and exhaustive category of eligible applicants.
results.classification_summary: This is a Call for Proposals issued by AICS (Agenzia Italiana per la Cooperazione allo Sviluppo) Nairobi for the PEACE initiative (AID 013184/01/1), targeting gender-based violence survivors in Kenya through mental health and psychosocial support at community level. The grant of EUR 1,500,000 is strictly reserved for Italian Civil Society Organizations (OSC) inscribed in the Italian AICS registry under Art. 26(3) of Law 125/2014. The ILO does not qualify as an eligible applicant under these criteria. The deadline is 27 April 2026, which is in the future (today is 22 April 2026).

ILO Eligibility.evidence: Section 9 (Criteri di eleggibilità dei partecipanti) and the overall framework explicitly restrict eligible applicants to Italian OSC (Organizzazioni della Società Civile) and non-profit entities registered on the AICS list under Art. 26, para. 3 of Law 125/2014. The ILO, as an international organization, is not included in this exhaustive list.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The CfP is a 'Bando affidato' (grant award) providing non-reimbursable funding (contributo/grant) of EUR 1,500,000 to an executing OSC for development cooperation activities.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The grant amount is EUR 1,500,000 (approximately USD 1,650,000), well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline for submission is 27 April 2026 at 16:00, which is in the future relative to today's date of 22 April 2026.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is a Call for Proposals (Bando affidato) for a development cooperation grant, not a procurement tender for goods or services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: No mandatory co-financing or cost-sharing requirement is mentioned in the available text.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: Applicants must be registered on the AICS list under Art. 26, comma 3 of Law 125/2014, which constitutes a specific pre-existing accreditation requirement.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The CfP requires an Italian OSC as the Soggetto Proponente (lead applicant), but does not specifically address UN/international organization consortium leadership restrictions beyond the general requirement for an Italian OSC lead.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The Soggetto Proponente must be an Italian OSC registered on the AICS list under Art. 26(3) of Law 125/2014, effectively restricting eligibility to Italian-registered organizations.

results.proposal_summary: The AICS (Italian Agency for Development Cooperation) Nairobi Regional Office has issued this Call for Proposals for the PEACE initiative (AID 013184/01/1 – 'Promuovere Equità, Ascolto, Consapevolezza ed Engagement Comunitario'), aimed at strengthening community-based mental health and psychosocial support services for gender-based violence survivors in Kenya. The grant of EUR 1,500,000 (part of a broader EUR 4,000,000 initiative) is exclusively open to Italian civil society organizations (OSC) registered under Art. 26(3) of Law 125/2014, who must partner with at least one local Kenyan organization. Activities are to be implemented in one of five target counties (Busia, Kilifi, Kisumu, Mombasa, or Nyeri). The submission deadline is 27 April 2026 at 16:00. results.exclusion_reason: Deadline has passed (29 March 2026) and grant size below threshold (each lot is €20,000, total €60,000 but individual lots are below USD 50,000). Additionally, this appears to be a procurement/service contract for consultancy studies rather than a project grant, and the eligible applicants are contractors/service providers, not international organizations like ILO.
results.classification_summary: This Call for Proposals from AICS Jerusalem Office seeks contractors to conduct three studies on Palestinian MSMEs under project AID 11399. Each lot is valued at €20,000. The submission deadline was 29 March 2026, which has passed. The call is structured as a service contract/procurement for consultancy studies rather than a grant. The ILO is not among eligible applicants as this is a tender for service providers.

Eligible applicants are contractors providing analytical services, not international organizations. The Terms of Reference explicitly reference 'contractors' and 'service contracts', not grant recipients. ILO as a UN agency does not fit the eligible applicant category.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The instrument is a service contract ('Service Contract' is explicitly mentioned in the ToR), not a grant. The contracting authority procures analytical studies from service providers, creating a commercial relationship rather than a non-reimbursable grant.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: Each of the three lots is valued at €20,000 (total €60,000). Individual lot values of €20,000 are below the USD 50,000 threshold. Even combined, this is a service contract, not a grant.

Deadline is future.evidence: The submission deadline stated is 29-03-2026 at 16:00, which is before today's date of 2026-04-22. The deadline has passed.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is clearly a procurement/service contract tender. The ToR explicitly references 'Service Contract', 'contractor(s)', and the call is structured for contracting service providers to deliver analytical studies, not a grant for project implementation.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: No co-financing requirement is mentioned in the call or Terms of Reference.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: No specific accreditation with external bodies is required. Requirements focus on technical expertise and qualifications of the team.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: No specific consortium leadership rules are mentioned in the available documents.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The call for proposal main document (call_for_proposal.pdf) could not be fully parsed (only 217 chars extracted). The ToR does not explicitly restrict by nationality, though it notes familiarity with Palestinian context as an advantage.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS) Jerusalem Office is seeking contractors to conduct three evidence-based studies under the project 'Enhancing the Business Environment for Palestinian MSMEs (EPEC) - AID 11399'. The studies cover: (1) the role of local institutions in private sector development, (2) women's economic empowerment in the green economy, and (3) characteristics of Palestinian informal enterprises. Each study constitutes a separate lot valued at €20,000 (total €60,000), structured as service contracts. The work is to be conducted in Palestinian Territories (West Bank, East Jerusalem, and Gaza) over a maximum of 6 months. Eligible applicants are consultancy firms and expert teams with relevant analytical expertise. The submission deadline was 29 March 2026 at 16:00 and has passed. No co-financing is required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The studies touch on ILO-relevant themes including informal economy, women's economic empowerment, MSME development, and labour markets in Palestinian Territories, but the instrument is a service procurement contract rather than a grant aligned with ILO mandate delivery.

results.exclusion_reason: Deadline has passed: the submission deadline was 09-04-2026 at 14:00, which is before today's date of 2026-04-22. Additionally, the call explicitly targets non-profit entities (soggetti non profit), which excludes the ILO as an international organization.
results.classification_summary: This is a Call for Proposals issued by AICS (Italian Agency for Development Cooperation), Tunis Office, for a humanitarian response programme supporting Sahrawi refugees in Algeria (Tindouf camps). The grant amount is EUR 2,000,000 (with EUR 1,800,000 for project activities and EUR 200,000 for management costs). The call is exclusively open to non-profit entities (soggetti non profit), and the submission deadline was 09 April 2026 at 14:00 — which has already passed as of today (2026-04-22). The ILO is ineligible on two grounds: (1) the deadline has passed, and (2) eligible applicants are restricted to non-profit organizations, not international/intergovernmental organizations.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The CfP explicitly states it selects projects from 'soggetti non profit' (non-profit entities), and the eligibility section (paragraphs 5.1 and 5.2) distinguishes between non-profit entities registered with AICS and those not registered with AICS. International organizations such as the ILO are not listed as eligible applicants. The call is strictly for non-profit entities.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The call is for a non-reimbursable contribution ('contributo a dono') totalling EUR 2,000,000, a standard grant instrument under Italian development cooperation law.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: The total fund is EUR 2,000,000 (with EUR 1,800,000 for project activities), well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The submission deadline was 09 April 2026 at 14:00 (Tunisian time, GMT+1). Today is 2026-04-22, so the deadline has already passed.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is a Call for Proposals for project grants to non-profit organizations, not a procurement tender or request for services.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: No mandatory co-financing or cost-sharing requirement is mentioned in the available text of the call.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: Applicants must either be registered ('iscritti') on the AICS list of non-profit organizations or meet specific alternative requirements. Being registered on the AICS list constitutes a form of pre-existing accreditation with the donor body.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The call mentions consortium (ATS - Associazione Temporanea di Scopo) arrangements are possible, but no specific rule requiring leadership by a non-UN entity is mentioned.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: The call does not explicitly restrict applicants to Italian-registered organizations only; it references AICS-registered non-profits but does not limit to domestic applicants exclusively.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), Tunis Office, issued a Call for Proposals on 23 February 2026 for the 'Programma di risposta umanitaria integrata per la popolazione rifugiata Sahrawi' (AID 013382), a humanitarian emergency programme targeting Sahrawi refugees in the Tindouf camps in Algeria. The total available funding is EUR 2,000,000 (EUR 1,800,000 for project activities in food security, health, education, and WASH; EUR 200,000 for management costs). The call is open exclusively to non-profit entities (soggetti non profit), either registered on the AICS list or meeting equivalent alternative requirements; consortia (ATS) are permitted. The programme is aligned with the Sahrawi Refugee Response Plan (SRRP) 2026–2027. The submission deadline was 9 April 2026 at 14:00 (Tunisian time). No co-financing requirement is specified. The ILO is ineligible as the deadline has passed and only non-profit organizations are eligible applicants.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While the programme addresses humanitarian needs in refugee camps, its focus on food security, health, WASH, and education has minimal direct alignment with the ILO's core mandate of labour standards, employment, and social protection; furthermore, ILO is categorically ineligible.

results.classification_summary: This is a delegated cooperation grant call by AICS (Italian Agency for Development Cooperation) under the NDICI AFRICA programme, for primary healthcare provision to vulnerable populations affected by food insecurity and malnutrition in Sudan. The call has two lots (€4M and €800K). The NDICI eligibility rules explicitly include international organisations, meaning the ILO as an international organisation is eligible. The deadline is 22 May 2026, which is in the future. The funding is non-reimbursable grant funding above the USD 50,000 threshold. However, the focus is on primary healthcare in a humanitarian crisis context (Sudan), which has limited alignment with ILO's core mandate on labour standards, employment, and social protection.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The NDICI eligibility document (a2a1_ecprogrammes_eligibility2021_2027_en.pdf) explicitly states that 'Participation in procurement and grant procedures for actions financed under geographic programmes... shall be open to international organisations and to all other legal entities...'. This call is funded under NDICI AFRICA/2023/441-988. ILO as an international organisation is explicitly included.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The call is explicitly titled 'Call for proposals' with grant awards (Lot 1: €4,000,000 and Lot 2: €800,000). The document references 'grant contract' and 'grant applicants', confirming non-reimbursable grant funding.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: Lot 1 is €4,000,000 and Lot 2 is €800,000, both well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: The deadline for submission is 22 May 2026 at 17:00 GMT+3, which is after today's date of 2026-04-22.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: This is a Call for Proposals for grants, not a procurement tender or RFP for services. The document explicitly refers to 'grant applicants' and 'grant contracts'.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: The guidelines document was truncated before reaching section 2.1.3 on eligible actions and cost eligibility rules. Co-financing requirements cannot be confirmed or excluded from available text.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: No specific pre-existing accreditation with a body such as GEF or Green Climate Fund is mentioned. Eligibility is based on NDICI programme rules for international organisations and legal entities.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The guidelines reference 'lead applicant and co-applicant(s)' but the specific rules on whether UN entities can lead consortia are not visible in the truncated guidelines text.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: NDICI eligibility is open to a broad range of countries including EU Member States, developing countries, and explicitly international organisations. There is no restriction to Italian-registered entities only.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS), acting under the EU NDICI AFRICA/2023/441-988 programme, has launched a delegated cooperation Call for Proposals (Grant/02/HealthPro II) for primary healthcare provision to vulnerable populations affected by food insecurity and malnutrition in Sudan. The call has two lots: Lot 1 (€4,000,000) and Lot 2 (€800,000), targeting interventions in Red Sea, Gedaref, White Nile, and Khartoum states in Sudan. The submission deadline is 22 May 2026 at 17:00 GMT+3. International organisations (including UN agencies such as ILO) and legal entities from NDICI-eligible countries are eligible to apply as lead or co-applicants. The call follows a two-step evaluation: concept notes are assessed first, followed by full application evaluation for pre-selected applicants. The programme responds to Sudan's severe humanitarian crisis, including near-total health system collapse, widespread famine, and disease outbreaks following the April 2023 conflict.
results.ilo_match_evidence: This call focuses on primary healthcare provision and nutrition in a humanitarian crisis in Sudan, which is outside ILO's core mandate areas of labour standards, employment, social protection policy, and social dialogue, though minor tangential links exist through crisis-affected decent work conditions.

results.proposal_summary: The Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS) Jerusalem Office issued a Call for Expression of Interest for a service contract to conduct an external evaluation of the EU-funded project 'Improvement of solid waste management in Dheisheh camp and the surrounding area' (NDICI-GEO-NEAR/2022/436-222, AID 012767) in Palestine. The evaluation is intended for May-June 2026 and will apply OECD-DAC criteria. This is a procurement/service contract (not a grant), open to local and international entities, companies, individuals, and organizations with at least 5 years of experience in external evaluations and documented experience in SWM or related fields, and prior working experience in Palestine or similar fragile/conflict-affected contexts. The submission deadline was March 9, 2026 at 10:00 am (Jerusalem time), which has passed. No contract value is stated. ILO is not eligible as this is a procurement tender for evaluation services, and the deadline has passed.
results.ilo_match_evidence: This call is for a procurement/service contract for external evaluation of a solid waste management project in a Palestinian refugee camp, which has minimal alignment with the ILO's core mandate on labour standards, employment, social protection, and decent work.

results.proposal_summary: The ASSIST Facility 2.0, managed by the Joint SDG Fund, is a blended finance investment vehicle targeting $150M to catalyse up to $10 billion in sustainable SDG-aligned investments in Indonesia by 2030. This is not a grant opportunity and is ineligible for ILO application.

results.proposal_summary: The TriNova 2026 Call for Triangular Cooperation Projects is a joint initiative by APC Colombia and Germany (BMZ/GIZ) that funds triangular cooperation projects of up to EUR 300,000 per project for a maximum duration of two years. The call is open to projects addressing any thematic area aligned with the 2030 Agenda and national development plans, involving at least one partner from Latin America, the Caribbean, or the Global South. Eligible applicants include public institutions, private sector entities, civil society organizations, academia, and NGOs — international organizations such as the ILO are not eligible. Projects must involve a requesting partner, a main partner, and TriNova as facilitating partner, with mandatory co-financing contributions (in kind or financial) from all partners. The deadline for submission is 08 May 2026, with proposals to be sent officially by email to both APC Colombia and GIZ.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While the call covers broad development themes with no sectoral restrictions — potentially touching on labour, skills, and decent work — ILO is explicitly ineligible as an international organization under the exhaustive applicant list, and the primary focus is on triangular cooperation modality rather than core ILO mandate areas.

results.proposal_summary: This ADB page describes a sovereign loan project (56216-001) approved in December 2024 for the ecological restoration and sustainable development of South Dongting Lake in Hunan Province, People's Republic of China. ADB is providing a loan of USD 150 million from ordinary capital resources, with an additional USD 65 million co-loan from Agence Française de Développement, for a total project cost of approximately USD 243 million. The project aims to enhance climate resilience and ecological security of wetland ecosystems in the South Dongting Lake basin, supporting nature reserve management, habitat restoration for waterbirds, climate-resilient livelihoods, and knowledge-sharing. The executing agencies are the Hunan Provincial Department of Finance and Yuanjiang Municipal Government. This is not a Call for Proposals — it is a sovereign lending operation with no grant window and no open application process for external organizations. The ILO is not an eligible applicant.
results.ilo_match_evidence: This project focuses on wetland ecological restoration and environmental sustainability in China, with only marginal relevance to ILO's core mandate areas of labour standards, employment, social protection, or decent work, though it tangentially touches on sustainable livelihoods and transitions to low-carbon production.

results.proposal_summary: This ADB project page describes the Aimags and Soums Green Regional Development Investment Program – Tranche 1 in Mongolia, a sovereign investment program approved on 22 March 2023 and effective from 19 March 2024 through 21 March 2029. The program aims to advance green and inclusive agro-territorial development in Mongolia's western aimags, focusing on climate-resilient urban/rural infrastructure, sustainable rangeland management, SME financing for livestock value chains, and institutional capacity building. Total financing exceeds USD 231 million, comprising ADB loans (USD 90M), Green Climate Fund loans and grants (USD 75.2M), European Investment Bank loans and grants (USD 63.4M), and ADB Asian Development Fund grants (USD 3M). The executing agency is Mongolia's Ministry of Economy and Development. This is not an open Call for Proposals — it is a sovereign lending/grant arrangement with the Government of Mongolia — and all listed consulting tenders are closed. The ILO cannot apply.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While the program touches on employment in agricultural/herder communities and rural economic development, it is primarily a climate, urban infrastructure, and agricultural investment program with no direct focus on ILO core themes such as labour standards, social protection, social dialogue, or decent work.

The project focuses on cultural and creative industries, participatory urban planning, and social inclusion in Bouaké.

results.proposal_summary: The Agence Française de Développement (AFD) has launched a call for initiatives for the implementation of an 'urban nursery' (Pépinière urbaine) in Bouaké, Côte d'Ivoire, as part of the broader Cultures en Côte d'Ivoire project. The total grant available is EUR 900,000, provided by AFD on a non-reimbursable basis, for a project to be implemented over a maximum of 36 months. The objective is to test and prefigure the cultural and social programming of the rehabilitated Centre Culturel Jacques Aka de Bouaké (CCJAB) through active participation of local residents, mobilization of stakeholders, and co-design of a temporary cultural space. Eligible applicants are strictly limited to national or international associations, NGOs, civil society organizations, and consortiums composed of non-profit structures and private-law companies. The submission deadline is 4 May 2026 at 12:00 Paris time. No co-financing is required.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The call focuses primarily on cultural and creative industries and urban participatory planning in Côte d'Ivoire, with only a secondary link to ILO themes through its mention of employment potential in cultural industries and women's economic empowerment, but it does not address core ILO mandates such as labour standards, social protection, or social dialogue.

results.proposal_summary: This URL points to the AFD (Agence Française de Développement) calls-for-projects listing page, which aggregates multiple calls across various themes including employment, decent work, gender equality, education, health, and social protection. As of 2026-04-22, all listed calls on this page are marked as 'Closed call' with deadlines that have passed (the most recent being May 6, 2025). No specific active or upcoming call is described with sufficient detail to perform a full eligibility assessment. The page is therefore not actionable as a current funding opportunity.
results.ilo_match_evidence: The AFD listing page includes themes such as Employment, Decent Work, and Social Protection that align with ILO mandate areas, but no specific active call targeting these themes is currently open.

results.exclusion_reason: ILO (as an International Organization/UN agency) is not eligible: the eligible applicant types are strictly limited to CSOs/NGOs (French associations, syndicates, foundations, local civil society organizations, and non-governmental organizations). Additionally, no single active call with a clear future deadline is identifiable — all specific FISONG and APCC calls referenced (2018, 2021, 2022) have deadlines well in the past relative to today (2026-04-22). The CSO Initiatives Facility opens mid-March to mid-May each year, but no 2026 call with a confirmed future deadline is documented in the source.
results.classification_summary: This AFD page describes multiple financing mechanisms for CSOs/NGOs (CSO Initiatives Facility, FISONG, APCC, FSOF, small-scale programs). Eligible applicants are strictly civil society organizations — French associations, foundations, syndicates, and local NGOs — explicitly excluding international organizations such as the ILO. All specific calls referenced have past deadlines. No active 2026 call with a future deadline is documented. The ILO fails both the ILO Eligibility and Deadline is future criteria.

ILO Eligibility.evidence: The page explicitly defines eligible applicants as French associations (Law 1901), French syndicates, French foundations, local CSOs in ODA-eligible countries, and NGOs. The ILO as a UN international organization does not fit any of these categories. FISONG Q&A also confirms 'A Non-Governmental Organization means any public interest organization which is not linked to a State or to an international institution,' explicitly excluding international institutions like the ILO.

Funding instrument is grant.evidence: The page describes co-financing via grants (subventions), with AFD providing non-reimbursable contributions covering up to 80–90% of project costs for CSOs.

Grant size above threshold.evidence: CSO Initiatives Facility requires total project amount ≥ €500,000; FISONG 2021 mentions projects of €700k–€1M per project, both well above the USD 50,000 threshold.

Deadline is future.evidence: All specific calls referenced have past deadlines: FISONG 2018 (deadline early 2018), FISONG 2020, FISONG 2021, FISONG 2022, APCC 2022, FSOF 2022. The CSO Initiatives Facility opens annually mid-March to mid-May but no confirmed 2026 call with a future deadline is documented in the source text. The FISONG was suspended in 2023. No active call with a deadline after 2026-04-22 is identified.

Is procurement or tender.evidence: The page describes grant mechanisms and calls for project proposals for CSOs, not procurement of goods/services or consultancy tenders.

Requires cofinancing.evidence: Co-financing is mandatory: FISONG requires NGOs to contribute at least 10% of the total project budget (and this cannot be in-kind). The CSO Initiatives Facility also specifies maximum AFD co-financing rates (80% for French CSOs, 90% for local CSOs), implying mandatory co-financing of at least 10–20%.

Requires specific accreditation.evidence: No specific pre-existing accreditation with a third-party body (e.g., GEF, Green Climate Fund) is required. Eligibility is based on organizational type, age, and track record.

Has consortium leadership rules.evidence: The lead applicant must be an NGO (non-governmental organization). FISONG Q&A confirms 'The lead applicant has to be a non-governmental organization (NGO),' which explicitly excludes UN/international organizations from leading consortia.

Applicant origin is restricted.evidence: While French CSOs are the primary target for the CSO Initiatives Facility, FISONG is open to international NGOs of any nationality, and the APCC is open to international CSOs. Geographic restriction does not apply uniformly across all mechanisms.

results.proposal_summary: The Agence Française de Développement (AFD), on behalf of the French Government, operates several grant financing mechanisms for civil society organizations (CSOs) and NGOs working in international solidarity and development. The main instruments include the CSO Initiatives Facility (for projects ≥ €500,000 in all OECD DAC ODA-eligible countries, co-financing up to 80–90% of project costs), the now-suspended FISONG innovation facility (open to international NGOs of any nationality, funding €700k–€1M per project), the Crisis and Post-Crisis Calls for Projects (APCC, funded via Minka Fund and EU trust funds), and the Support Fund for Feminist Organizations (FSOF). Eligible applicants are strictly CSOs/NGOs — French associations, syndicates, foundations, and local civil society organizations — explicitly excluding international organizations such as the ILO. All specific calls documented on this page have closed deadlines (2018–2022), and the FISONG has been suspended since 2023. The CSO Initiatives Facility reopens annually mid-March to mid-May but no confirmed 2026 active call is documented. Mandatory co-financing of at least 10% is required for most instruments.
results.ilo_match_evidence: While some past FISONG themes (social protection, financial inclusion of migrants, informal economy) align with ILO's mandate, the ILO is explicitly ineligible as an international institution under all AFD CSO financing mechanisms, making this opportunity irrelevant for ILO regardless of thematic alignment.
